title: Trump reinstates naval blockade on Iran and unleashes airstrikes, oil rises 10%
summary: The White House reimposes the blockade in the Persian Gulf with a 20% toll and orders bombings for three consecutive days; Iran responds with drone attacks, which surges crude oil and causes drops on Wall Street.

title: Subsidiary capital project advances in Diet with extension of session period
summary: The government and opposition negotiate modifications to the secondary capital project, while an extension of the legislative period is debated to pass key laws such as the subsidiary capital one.

title: Murakami debuts in All-Star home run contest with 9 homers
summary: The Japanese slugger of the Yakult Swallows participates for the first time in the MLB Home Run Derby, being eliminated in the first round with 9 homers in 20 swings.

title: United States intensifies pressure against the ICC and asks allies to withdraw
summary: The Donald Trump administration considers the International Criminal Court a threat and seeks its dismantling through threats of sanctions and diplomatic pressure.

title: Man sets fire to parents' home in Akita, causes two deaths
summary: Police arrest the 44-year-old son as the main suspect in the fire in Nikaho, where the parents remain incomunicated and are feared dead.

title: Five crew members fall into sea as rubber boat capsizes in Hokkaido; one dies
summary: The rubber craft capsizes off the coast of Wakkanai, leaving four survivors and one fatality in the accident.

title: Rintaro Sasaki drafted by Marlins in eighth round of MLB draft
summary: The young Japanese batter, former school baseball star, begins his professional career in the United States after being selected by Miami.

title: Japan approves second basic plan on artificial intelligence with emphasis on safety
summary: The cabinet decides to update the national AI strategy, addressing risks and promoting technological autonomy, in a context of rapid cyber changes.

title: Turkish man dies in police custody in Tokyo; authorities rule out homicide
summary: A man of Turkish nationality dies during his detention at the Takao police station; the autopsy determines it was from natural causes.

title: The sacred child of the Naginata float participates in the opening ceremony of Gion Matsuri
summary: The little 'chigo' rides a white horse in the Shasan no Gi ritual, marking the start of the festivities of the famous Kyoto festival.

title: Yutaka Take reaches 5,000 wins in his legendary career as a jockey
summary: The historic horse racing jockey achieves the milestone at his father's hometown racetrack, in front of nearly 7,000 fans.

title: Yoshinobu Yamamoto will not pitch in All-Star Game but plans to enjoy it
summary: The Dodgers' ace will not be used in the All-Star Game by team decision, although he is excited to experience it.

title: Hisatsu railway line resumes service between Yoshimatsu and Hayato
summary: The section resumes operation, allowing students to travel by train again and bringing life back to the line.

title: United States forms special team to catch document leaker
summary: The federal government intensifies the hunt for the person responsible for the intelligence leak, while increasing pressure on the media that published the information.

title: Hyogo prefecture issues bonds worth 33.8 billion yen in violation of the law
summary: The prefectural authority incurs an illegal debt issuance without knowing its illegality, sparking controversy.

title: Supreme Court recognizes legitimacy of three citizens to sue over Futenma relocation
summary: The court grants legal status to Okinawa residents to challenge the base construction in Henoko, in a significant ruling.

title: Ukraine and European countries form defense alliance against Russian ballistic missiles
summary: The coalition focuses on missile defense in response to the Russian threat, amid the ongoing war.

title: Japan to launch Disaster Management Agency in November with preventive focus
summary: The new government agency will be dedicated to disaster prevention and mitigation measures, although it faces challenges in personnel and effectiveness.

title: Professor warns that a crisis in Taiwan is not a natural disaster but an armed conflict
summary: A University of Tokyo academic urges the international community to prepare for a possible contingency in Taiwan as a serious geopolitical scenario.

title: Semiconductor memory stocks plunge 30% from their peak
summary: The memory chip sector suffers a sharp market correction, reflecting three main concerns weighing on the industry.
